title: Dar'Or

{{Quote|Because it is the last home of the elix, Dar'Or has been declared an Imperial Species Preservation Zone, and travel to and from Dar'Or is restricted.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
'''Dar'Or''', also known as '''OM973''', was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Jospro sector/Legends|Jospro sector]]. Its land surface was primarily covered in [[Forest/Legends|forests]] of two-hundred-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-tall [[waza]] [[Tree/Legends|trees]]. The forests, which had multiple floor-like levels, were home to the [[Diet/Legends|predatory]] [[indola]] tree-sloths as well as the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] flying [[Primate/Legends|primates]] [[Ri'Dar]].

At one point, a group of planetary [[Ecologist/Legends|ecologists]] transplanted the [[elix]] [[Bird/Legends|birds]] of prey from the [[Species/Legends|species']] [[Unidentified elix homeworld|doomed homeworld]] to Dar'Or. Due to the former world having had higher [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]], however, the powerful elix became a dangerous threat to the Ri'Dar. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] declared Dar'Or an [[Imperial Species Preservation Zone]], restricting travel to and from the planet. Nevertheless, [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] violated the restriction in order to smuggle elix [[Foodstuff/Legends|meat]] and live indola off-world, in the process also providing an avenue for individual Ri'Dar to leave their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Over most of its land, Dar'Or has a subtropical, humid climate with large forests of ''waza'' trees.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
[[File:JosproSector-EAOC.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Dar'Or was situated in the Jospro sector.]]
Dar'Or, designated as "OM973" in the [[Star map/Legends|star-charts]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], was a moderately sized [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Dar'Or system/Legends|Dar'Or system]], a part of the [[Jospro sector/Legends|Jospro sector]]<ref name="GG42" /> within [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Slice" /> It [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] an [[Color/Legends|orange]] [[Star/Legends|star]] and had low density and [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] pull.<ref name="GG42" />

The planet's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] was rich in [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]], [[Nitrogen/Legends|nitrogen]], and [[Carbon dioxide/Legends|carbon dioxide]], and the climate over most of Dar'Or's landmasses was subtropical and humid. [[Water/Legends|Water]] covered sixty percent of the planet's surface, and the rest of it featured expansive [[Forest/Legends|forests]] of [[waza]]—large coniferous [[Tree/Legends|trees]] reaching two hundred [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height. The canopies of the waza trees were so thick that they blocked sunlight, casting the floors of the forests—which were covered in giant [[Fungus/Legends|fungi]] instead of green [[Plant/Legends|plants]] as a result—into permanent shadow even on the brightest of Dar'Or's [[Local day/Legends|days]].<ref name="GG42" />

In the waza forests' middle elevations, an entire distinct ecosystem developed due to networks of [[baloo vine]]s catching and trapping falling needles of the large conifers. Over the span of [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]], the decomposing of the needles on the baloo levels led to the formation of a thick humus capable of supporting small plants. Consequently, the waza forests themselves appeared to have multiple different levels of forest floor. Other examples of Dar'Or's flora included plants that bore [[Berry/Legends|berries]], [[Fruit/Legends|fruits]], and [[Nut/Legends|nuts]]. Dar'Or's wildlife included the [[Diet/Legends|predatory]] tree-sloths known as [[indola]] as well as flying [[Insect/Legends|insects]], [[Lizard/Legends|lizards]], and [[Rodent/Legends|rodents]].<ref name="GG42" />

==History==
{{Quote|The question of just what exactly is occurring on Dar'Or remains unanswered. No one understands why the Empire has put so much effort in isolating this planet from the galaxy.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
[[File:Ridar.jpg|thumb|right|350px|The Ri'Dar were native to Dar'Or.]]
At some point, a group of zealous planetary [[Ecologist/Legends|ecologists]] attempted to save the [[elix]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of small [[Bird/Legends|birds]] of prey [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to a [[Unidentified elix homeworld|forested world]] that was about to be destroyed by a [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]]. The ecologists transplanted the elix on Dar'Or; however, they overlooked the fact that the new home of the elix had lower gravity, which led to the avians becoming powerful and fast apex predators that threatened the native [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]], the [[Ri'Dar]].<ref name="GG42" />

By the [[Time/Legends|time]] shortly following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="SWR">[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']]</ref> the Galactic Empire declared Dar'Or an [[Imperial Species Preservation Zone]] and restricted travel to and from the planet. It was ostensibly done to protect the last home of the elix; however, it was not well-understood why the Empire put so much effort into isolating Dar'Or from the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and certain individuals wondered what exactly did the government's interests on the planet entail. In addition, despite the travel restrictions, [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] occasionally visited Dar'Or,<ref name="GG42" /> where they [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] the elix<ref name="CSWE Dar'Or">{{CSWECite|I|170|Dar'Or}}</ref> in order to sell their [[Foodstuff/Legends|meat]] to gourmets of the wider [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] and sometimes transported the indola creatures offworld for animal trainers and [[Collector/Legends|collectors]].<ref name="GG42" />

Several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the elix had been introduced on Dar'Or, the [[sentientologist]] [[Obo Rin]] included the planet and the Ri'Dar in the ''[[Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy]]''. In that work, Rin described the sentient species that [[Gender/Legends|he]] considered to be the most important in the galaxy and of the most interest to the Empire.<ref name="GG42" /> When the [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] [[To'iir]] consulted the [[Trader's Gamble]] feature of the [[Lantillian Spacers' Brotherhood/Legends|Lantillian Spacers' Brotherhood's]] [[Free-Trader's Guide]] service for a possible hiding place from Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Roff]]'s forces, the service listed Dar'Or among fourteen planets with limited [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] facilities in the Slice portion of the Outer Rim. After reading through the list, To'iir decided he and his partner, [[Liadden]], would travel to the world [[Gamorr/Legends|Gamorr]].<ref name="Journal10">{{JournalCite|10|A Free-Trader's Guide to the Planets}}</ref>

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Ri'Dar}}
{{Quote|The Ri'Dar are flying, tree-dwelling primates approximately one meter in length.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
Dar'Or was the homeworld of the sentient Ri'Dar, a species of flying, tree-dwelling [[Primate/Legends|primates]]. Their [[technology level]] was primitive, consisting primarily of [[Wood/Legends|wooden]] [[Spear/Legends|spears]], [[Bow/Legends|bows]], and [[Arrow/Legends|arrows]] as well as simple dwellings, and the species constantly fought for survival against their homeworld's natural predators. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], an increasing number of Ri'Dar traveled off-world, primarily leaving Dar'Or aboard smuggler [[Starship/Legends|starships]] visiting the planet.<ref name="GG42" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|All of the families inhabiting a single waza tree comprise a warren, and all of the warrens grouped together in a particular section of forest make a city.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
Dar'Or's native Ri'Dar lived in [[City/Legends|cities]] consisting of several waza trees, of which each was home to a single warren of Ri'Dar families.<ref name="GG42" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Gg4-1.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Dar'Or was introduced in ''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races''.]]
Dar'Or was first mentioned in ''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'', a [[1989]] sourcebook authored by [[Troy Denning]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG41">''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]''</ref> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], spelled the planet's Imperial star-[[Map/Legends|chart]] designation as "OM 973." Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process, its [[canon]]icity within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity was never confirmed.<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref>

Dar'Or's first full appearance came in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], which also provided the planet's only visual depiction—an image also used to illustrate twenty-one other world in the game.<ref name="SWR" /> The game's accompanying [[Prima Games]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] misspelled Dar'Or's name as "Dar'or."<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Rebellion'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Dar'Or [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square R-7.<ref name="TEA" />

==Appearances==
*[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] {{1st}}
